title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement [INSERT CONTACT METHOD]. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Generate a standard load profile
text: two generations profiles come different source files use different units: 1999 profiles (H0, G0–G6, L0–L2) published Excel file every value already expressed average electric power watts (W), normalised annual sum 15-minute intervals equals 1,000 kWh.3 2025 profiles (H25, G25, L25, P25, S25) published separate Excel file every value expressed energy consumed 15-minute interval kilowatt-hours (kWh), normalised annual consumption 1,000,000 kWh.4 give users single, consistent interface convert values watts normalised 1,000 kWh/. conversion applied , data-build time, data-raw/DATASET.R. result, slp_generate() always returns watts regardless profile requested. can verify normalisation holds 2025 profile just 1999 profile:

title: Generate a standard load profile
text: values returned slp_generate() represent average electric power 15-minute interval. obtain energy consumed interval kWh can wrap slp_generate() :
code: slp_generate_kwh <- \(...) {   out <- slp_generate(...)   out$kwh <- out$watts / 4 / 1000   out }  H0_kwh <- slp_generate_kwh("H0", "2026-01-01", "2026-12-31") sum(H0_kwh$kwh) #> [1] 998.1163

title: Generate a standard load profile
text: call slp_generate(), generate (surprise!) standard load profile. steps performed: Generate date sequence start_date end_date. Map day combination day period (1999 profiles: seasonal period; 2025 profiles: calendar month). Use result 2nd step extract values slp.5 Apply polynomial function values profile identifiers H0, H25, P25, S25. Return data.

title: Generate a standard load profile
text: initial step, date sequence created start_date end_date based user input. ’s simple example:

what: Map each day to a period and a weekday
title: Generate a standard load profile
text: measured load profiles analysed study showed electricity consumption across groups fluctuates period year days within week. 1999 profiles, period definition : summer: May 15 September 14 winter: November 1 March 20 transition: March 21 May 14, September 15 October 31 2025 profiles, calendar month treated period (january … december) rather grouping months seasons. 1999 study also found significant difference consumption weekdays Monday Friday group. reason, days Monday Friday grouped together workday. December 24th 31st considered Saturdays Sundays. Public holidays regarded Sundays. Note: function slp_generate() supports default nationwide public holidays Germany. retrieved nager.Date API: New Year’s (Jan 1) Good Friday Easter Monday Labour Day (May 1) Ascension Day Whit Monday German Unity Day (Oct 3) Christmas Day (Dec 25) Boxing Day (Dec 26) State-level holidays included default, vary state can change time. Use optional holidays argument pass vector dates take full control dates treated public holidays; built-data ignored entirely. See README example fetch state-level holidays nager.Date API pass slp_generate(). result second step mapping date -called characteristic profile day, .e. combination weekday period:
code: wkday_period <- standardlastprofile:::get_wkday_period(date_seq) data.frame(input = date_seq, output = wkday_period) #>        input          output #> 1 2023-12-22  workday_winter #> 2 2023-12-23 saturday_winter #> 3 2023-12-24   sunday_winter #> 4 2023-12-25   sunday_winter #> 5 2023-12-26   sunday_winter #> 6 2023-12-27  workday_winter

what: Assign consumption values to each day
title: Generate a standard load profile
text: third step assign measurements know slp dataset characteristic profile day. job slp_generate() function: function returns data frame 4 columns: data analysis revealed load fluctuations commercial agricultural customers remain moderate throughout year. Specifically, customers customer groups labelled G0 G6 L0 L2, standard load profile can accurately derived nine characteristic profile day combinations (3 day types × 3 seasonal periods) available dataset slp. code snippet README, can used reproduce plot G5 profile, showcasing algorithm’s outcome:  can see, values 2023 December 24 (Sunday) December 25 26 (public holidays) identical.
code: G5 <- slp_generate(   profile_id = "G5",   start_date = "2023-12-22",   end_date = "2023-12-27"   ) head(G5) #>   profile_id          start_time            end_time watts #> 1         G5 2023-12-22 00:00:00 2023-12-22 00:15:00  50.1 #> 2         G5 2023-12-22 00:15:00 2023-12-22 00:30:00  47.4 #> 3         G5 2023-12-22 00:30:00 2023-12-22 00:45:00  44.9 #> 4         G5 2023-12-22 00:45:00 2023-12-22 01:00:00  43.3 #> 5         G5 2023-12-22 01:00:00 2023-12-22 01:15:00  43.0 #> 6         G5 2023-12-22 01:15:00 2023-12-22 01:30:00  43.8 library(ggplot2) ggplot(G5, aes(start_time, watts)) +   geom_line(color = "#0CC792") +   scale_x_datetime(     date_breaks = "1 day",     date_labels = "%b %d") +   scale_y_continuous(NULL, labels = \(x) paste(x, "W")) +   labs(     title = "'G5': bakery with bakehouse",     subtitle = "1/4h measurements, based on consumption of 1,000 kWh/a",     caption = "data: www.bdew.de",     x = NULL) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(     panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),     panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),     panel.grid = element_line(       linetype = "12",       lineend = "round",       colour = "#FAF6F4"       )   ) +   NULL

what: Special case: H0, H25, P25, S25
title: Generate a standard load profile
text: contrast commercial agricultural businesses, relatively even constant electricity consumption throughout year, household electricity consumption decreases winter summer vice versa (least Germany). distinctive annual load profile characteristics household customers, contend customers adequately described static representation using characteristic days alone. Consequently, values slp dataset H0, H25, P25, S25 serve base values scaled dynamization factor. taken account call slp_generate(). study suggested application 4th order polynomial function values profiles. wd=ws×(−3.92e−10×d4+3.20e−7×d3−7.02e−5×d2+2.10e−3×d+1.24) w_d = w_s \times(-3.92\mathrm{e}{-10} \times d^4 + 3.20\mathrm{e}{-7} \times  d^3 - 7.02\mathrm{e}{-5} \times d^2 + 2.10\mathrm{e}{-3} \times d + 1.24)  : wdw_d resulting ‘dynamic’ value wsw_s ‘static’ value dd day year integer, starting 1 January 1st following plot shows electrical power develops year profile H0; clearer picture, values aggregated daily level:  dynamization step produces representative, dynamic load profile. Finally, following chart compares dynamic values static counterparts.6

what: BDEW Standard Load Profiles for Electricity
title: BDEW Standard Load Profiles for Electricity
text: package provides data standard load profiles electricity, published German Association Energy Water Industries (BDEW Bundesverband der Energie- und Wasserwirtschaft e.V.).

title: BDEW Standard Load Profiles for Electricity
text: can install standardlastprofile CRAN : install development version GitHub use:

title: BDEW Standard Load Profiles for Electricity
text: slp — dataset containing BDEW standard load profiles electricity. slp_generate() — generate standard load profile user-defined time period. slp_info() — retrieve details standard load profiles.

title: BDEW Standard Load Profiles for Electricity
text: dataset slp contains 26,784 observations across 5 variables: profile_id: identifier standard load profile period: one "summer", "winter", "transition" 1999 profiles; lowercase month name ("january" … "december") 2025 profiles day: one "workday", "saturday", "sunday" timestamp: format "%H:%M" watts: electric power context German electricity market, term Standard Load Profile denotes representative pattern electricity consumption specific period. profiles portray anticipated electricity consumption diverse customer groups, like households businesses. exact match individual customer’s consumption profile, serve valid approximation larger groups similar customers. unique combination profile_id, period day 96 x 1/4 hour measurements watts. dataset covers two generations profiles: 1999 profiles1 — based analysis 1,209 load profiles low-voltage electricity consumers Germany: H0: households (German: “Haushalte”) G0 G6: commerce (“Gewerbe”) L0 L2: agriculture (“Landwirtschaft”) 2025 profiles — updated set published BDEW reflecting changes consumption patterns: H25, G25, L25: updated household, commerce, agriculture profiles P25: households photovoltaic (PV) system S25: households PV system battery storage details, call slp_info() function.
code: library(standardlastprofile) str(slp) #> 'data.frame':    26784 obs. of  5 variables: #>  $ profile_id: chr  "H0" "H0" "H0" "H0" ... #>  $ period    : chr  "winter" "winter" "winter" "winter" ... #>  $ day       : chr  "saturday" "saturday" "saturday" "saturday" ... #>  $ timestamp : chr  "00:00" "00:15" "00:30" "00:45" ... #>  $ watts     : num  70.8 68.2 65.9 63.3 59.5 55 50.5 46.6 43.9 42.3 ... slp_info(profile_id = "H0", language = "DE") #> $H0 #> $H0$profile #> [1] "H0" #>  #> $H0$description #> [1] "Haushalt" #>  #> $H0$details #> [1] "In dieses Profil werden alle Haushalte mit ausschließlichem und überwiegendem Privatverbrauch eingeordnet. Haushalte mit überwiegend privatem elektrischen Verbrauch, d.h. auch mit geringfügigem gewerblichen Bedarf sind z.B. Handelsvertreter, Heimarbeiter u.ä. mit Büro im Haushalt. Das Profil Haushalt ist nicht anwendbar bei Sonderanwendungen wie z.B. elektrischen Speicherheizungen oder Wärmepumpen."

title: BDEW Standard Load Profiles for Electricity
text: 2025, BDEW published updated set standard load profiles reflecting changes electricity consumption patterns since original 1999 study. Five new profiles included: H25: households — updated version H0 G25: commerce (general) — updated version G0 L25: agriculture — updated version L0 P25: combination profile households photovoltaic (PV) system S25: combination profile households PV system battery storage Unlike 1999 profiles, group days three seasonal periods (winter, summer, transition), 2025 profiles provide separate set values calendar month. P25 S25 entirely new profile types 1999 equivalent, capturing growing role distributed generation storage residential electricity consumption.  chart places 2025 household profiles side side H0 reference, showing cumulative energy consumption diverges course year.  H25 tracks H0 almost exactly, confirming updated household profile represents similar consumption pattern 1999 predecessor. P25 S25, hand, show flattening cumulative curve spring summer: households photovoltaic system — even additional battery storage — draw less energy grid months high solar yield.

title: BDEW Standard Load Profiles for Electricity
text: create standard load profile specified time period, call slp_generate() function:

title: BDEW Standard Load Profiles for Electricity
text: default, slp_generate() treats nine public holidays observed nationwide Germany Sundays: New Year’s (Jan 1) Good Friday Easter Monday Labour Day (May 1) Ascension Day Whit Monday German Unity Day (Oct 3) Christmas Day (Dec 25) Boxing Day (Dec 26) State-level holidays included vary state can change time. Use holidays argument supply dates — built-data ignored entirely. example fetches 2027 public holidays Germany state Berlin nager.Date API, passes slp_generate(). Berlin observes International Women’s Day (8 March) additional public holiday shared state. generate standard load profile including holidays 16 German states, repeat pattern loop — one API call suffices, re-filter per state: information, details data, explanation algorithm, see vignette run vignette("standardlastprofile", package = "standardlastprofile") locally.
code: library(httr2)  resp <- request("https://date.nager.at") |>   req_url_path_append("api", "v3", "PublicHolidays", "2027", "DE") |>   req_perform() |>   resp_body_json()  # global == TRUE  →  nationwide holiday (counties is NULL) # global == FALSE →  counties lists the states that observe it is_berlin <- \(x) isTRUE(x$global) || "DE-BE" %in% unlist(x$counties)  holidays_berlin_2027 <- as.Date(   vapply(Filter(is_berlin, resp), \(x) x$date, character(1)) )  H0_berlin_2027 <- slp_generate(   "H0", "2027-01-01", "2027-12-31",   holidays = holidays_berlin_2027 ) states <- c(   "DE-BB", "DE-BE", "DE-BW", "DE-BY", "DE-HB", "DE-HE",   "DE-HH", "DE-MV", "DE-NI", "DE-NW", "DE-RP", "DE-SH",   "DE-SL", "DE-SN", "DE-ST", "DE-TH" )  results <- vector("list", length(states)) names(results) <- states  for (state in states) {   is_state <- \(x) isTRUE(x$global) || state %in% unlist(x$counties)   holidays_state <- as.Date(     vapply(Filter(is_state, resp), \(x) x$date, character(1))   )   results[[state]] <- slp_generate(     profile_id = "H0",     start_date = "2027-01-01",     end_date = "2027-12-31",     holidays = holidays_state   ) }

title: Standard Load Profile Data for Electricity from BDEW — slp
Standard Load Profile Data for Electricity from BDEW — slp
text: data.frame 26,784 observations 5 variables: profile_id character, identifier load profile, see 'Details' period character, one 'summer', 'winter', 'transition' 1999 profiles; one 'january' 'december' 2025 profiles day character, one 'saturday', 'sunday', 'workday' timestamp character, format: %H:%M watts numeric, electric power watts, normalised 1,000 kWh/

title: Standard Load Profile Data for Electricity from BDEW — slp
text: https://www.bdew.de/energie/standardlastprofile-strom/ https://www.bdew.de/media/documents/Profile.zip https://www.bdew.de/media/documents/1999_Repraesentative-VDEW-Lastprofile.pdf

title: Standard Load Profile Data for Electricity from BDEW — slp
text: 96 x 1/4h measurements electrical power combination profile_id, period day, refer "standard load profile". total 16 profile_id across two generations profiles: 1999 profiles (based analysis 1,209 load profiles low-voltage electricity consumers Germany): Households: H0 Commercial: G0, G1, G2, G3, G4, G5, G6 Agriculture: L0, L1, L2 2025 profiles (updated profiles published BDEW 2025): Households: H25 Commercial: G25 Agriculture: L25 Combination profile PV: P25 Combination profile storage PV: S25 2025 profiles use calendar months rather seasons period column ('january' 'december'). Call slp_info() information examples. Period definitions (1999 profiles): summer: May 15 September 14 winter: November 1 March 20 transition: March 21 May 14, September 15 October 31 Day definitions: workday: Monday Friday saturday: Saturdays; Dec 24th Dec 31st also treated Saturdays unless fall Sunday sunday: Sundays public holidays Units normalisation: source Excel file 1999 profiles stores values watts (W), normalised annual consumption 1,000 kWh/. source Excel file 2025 profiles stores values kilowatt-hours (kWh) per 15-minute interval, normalised 1,000,000 kWh/. keep internal representation consistent backwards compatible, 2025 values converted watts normalised 1,000 kWh/. result, watts column dataset output slp_generate() always represents average electric power watts, normalised 1,000 kWh/. convert energy consumed per 15-minute interval kWh, divide 4 1,000:

title: Standard Load Profile Data for Electricity from BDEW — slp
code: head(slp) #>   profile_id period      day timestamp watts #> 1         H0 winter saturday     00:00  70.8 #> 2         H0 winter saturday     00:15  68.2 #> 3         H0 winter saturday     00:30  65.9 #> 4         H0 winter saturday     00:45  63.3 #> 5         H0 winter saturday     01:00  59.5 #> 6         H0 winter saturday     01:15  55.0

what: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Electricity — slp_generate
title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Electricity — slp_generate
text: Generate standard load profile watts, normalised annual consumption 1,000 kWh.

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Electricity — slp_generate
code: slp_generate(   profile_id,   start_date,   end_date,   holidays = NULL,   state_code = deprecated() )

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Electricity — slp_generate
text: https://www.bdew.de/energie/standardlastprofile-strom/ https://www.bdew.de/media/documents/1999_Repraesentative-VDEW-Lastprofile.pdf https://www.bdew.de/media/documents/2000131_Anwendung-repraesentativen_Lastprofile-Step--step.pdf

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Electricity — slp_generate
text: profile_id load profile identifier, required start_date start date ISO 8601 format, greater equal "1990-01-01", required end_date end date ISO 8601 format, later "2073-12-31", required holidays optional character Date vector dates ISO 8601 format ("YYYY-MM-DD") treated public holidays (therefore mapped "sunday" algorithm). supplied, built-holiday data ignored entirely dates holidays used. state_code Use holidays instead.

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Electricity — slp_generate
text: data.frame four variables: profile_id, character, load profile identifier start_time, POSIXct / POSIXlt, start time end_time, POSIXct / POSIXlt, end time watts, numeric, average electric power watts per 15-minute interval, normalised annual consumption 1,000 kWh

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Electricity — slp_generate
text: German electricity market, standard load profile representative pattern electricity consumption used forecast demand customer groups continuously metered. distinct combination profile_id, period, day 96 quarter-hourly measurements electrical power, normalised annual consumption 1,000 kWh. function supports data 1990 2073. See vignette("standardlastprofile") details algorithm.

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Electricity — slp_generate
text: 16 profile IDs across two generations: 1999 profiles: H0: Households G0, G1, G2, G3, G4, G5, G6: Commercial L0, L1, L2: Agriculture 2025 profiles 2025, BDEW published updated set standard load profiles reflecting changes electricity consumption patterns since original 1999 study. Five new profiles included: H25: households — updated version H0 G25: commerce (general) — updated version G0 L25: agriculture — updated version L0 P25: combination profile households photovoltaic (PV) system S25: combination profile households PV system battery storage descriptions profile, call slp_info().

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Electricity — slp_generate
text: 1999 profiles use three seasonal periods: summer: May 15 September 14 winter: November 1 March 20 transition: March 21 May 14, September 15 October 31 2025 profiles use calendar months (january … december) instead seasons. Within period, days classified : workday: Monday Friday saturday: Saturdays; Dec 24th Dec 31st also treated Saturdays unless fall Sunday sunday: Sundays public holidays

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Electricity — slp_generate
text: default, following nine public holidays observed nationwide across German states treated Sundays: New Year's Day (1 January) Good Friday Easter Monday Labour Day (1 May) Ascension Day Whit Monday German Unity Day (3 October) Christmas Day (25 December) Boxing Day (26 December) State-level holidays included default. vary state can change — example, Berlin observed one-time holiday 8 May 2025 (end World War II anniversary). Use holidays argument supply dates instead; built-data ignored entirely.

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Electricity — slp_generate
text: 1999 source file stores values watts (W), normalised 1,000 kWh/. 2025 source file stores values kWh per 15-minute interval, normalised 1,000,000 kWh/. keep profiles consistent, 2025 values converted watts normalised 1,000 kWh/. convert energy consumed per interval kWh:

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Electricity — slp_generate
code: start <- "2026-01-01" end <- "2026-12-31"  # multiple profile IDs are supported L <- slp_generate(c("L0", "L1", "L2"), start, end) head(L) #>   profile_id          start_time            end_time watts #> 1         L0 2026-01-01 00:00:00 2026-01-01 00:15:00  68.3 #> 2         L0 2026-01-01 00:15:00 2026-01-01 00:30:00  66.0 #> 3         L0 2026-01-01 00:30:00 2026-01-01 00:45:00  64.3 #> 4         L0 2026-01-01 00:45:00 2026-01-01 01:00:00  63.0 #> 5         L0 2026-01-01 01:00:00 2026-01-01 01:15:00  62.1 #> 6         L0 2026-01-01 01:15:00 2026-01-01 01:30:00  61.4  # supply custom holiday dates (e.g. only treat New Year's Day as a holiday) H0_custom <- slp_generate("H0", start, end, holidays = "2026-01-01")  # Fetch state-level holidays from the nager.Date API and pass them in. # Each entry in the API response contains two relevant fields: #   $global  — logical; TRUE = nationwide holiday, FALSE = state-specific #   $counties — list of ISO 3166-2 state codes (e.g. "DE-BE" for Berlin) #               when global is FALSE; NULL otherwise # # Berlin (DE-BE) observes International Women's Day (March 8) in addition # to all nationwide holidays. The example below fetches 2027 holidays, # keeps entries where global is TRUE or "DE-BE" appears in counties, and # passes the resulting dates to slp_generate(). if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ resp <- httr2::request("https://date.nager.at/api/v3") |>   httr2::req_url_path_append("PublicHolidays", "2027", "DE") |>   httr2::req_perform() |>   httr2::resp_body_json()  is_berlin <- \(x) isTRUE(x$global) || "DE-BE" %in% unlist(x$counties) holidays_berlin_2027 <- as.Date(   vapply(Filter(is_berlin, resp), \(x) x$date, character(1)) )  H0_berlin_2027 <- slp_generate(   "H0", "2027-01-01", "2027-12-31",   holidays = holidays_berlin_2027 ) } # }  # consider only nationwide public holidays (default) H0_2026 <- slp_generate("H0", start, end)  # when the deprecated state_code and holidays are both supplied, both sets # of dates are treated as Sundays: user-provided dates from holidays and # state-specific built-in holidays from state_code are merged suppressWarnings(   slp_generate("G0", "2026-04-01", "2026-04-01",     state_code = "SL", holidays = "2026-04-01") |>     head() ) #>   profile_id          start_time            end_time watts #> 1         G0 2026-04-01 00:00:00 2026-04-01 00:15:00  68.3 #> 2         G0 2026-04-01 00:15:00 2026-04-01 00:30:00  66.5 #> 3         G0 2026-04-01 00:30:00 2026-04-01 00:45:00  64.6 #> 4         G0 2026-04-01 00:45:00 2026-04-01 01:00:00  62.6 #> 5         G0 2026-04-01 01:00:00 2026-04-01 01:15:00  60.3 #> 6         G0 2026-04-01 01:15:00 2026-04-01 01:30:00  57.9  # electric power values are normalised to consumption of ~1,000 kWh/a sum(H0_2026$watts / 4 / 1000) #> [1] 998.1163  # convert watts to kWh per interval using a wrapper slp_generate_kwh <- \(...) {   out <- slp_generate(...)   out$kwh <- out$watts / 4 / 1000   out } H0_kwh <- slp_generate_kwh("H0", start, end) head(H0_kwh) #>   profile_id          start_time            end_time     watts        kwh #> 1         H0 2026-01-01 00:00:00 2026-01-01 00:15:00 108.67764 0.02716941 #> 2         H0 2026-01-01 00:15:00 2026-01-01 00:30:00 100.72864 0.02518216 #> 3         H0 2026-01-01 00:30:00 2026-01-01 00:45:00  93.15226 0.02328806 #> 4         H0 2026-01-01 00:45:00 2026-01-01 01:00:00  85.82428 0.02145607 #> 5         H0 2026-01-01 01:00:00 2026-01-01 01:15:00  78.74471 0.01968618 #> 6         H0 2026-01-01 01:15:00 2026-01-01 01:30:00  72.28615 0.01807154

what: Retrieve information on standard load profiles — slp_info
title: Retrieve information on standard load profiles — slp_info
text: Information examples standard load profiles German Association Energy Water Industries (BDEW Bundesverband der Energie- und Wasserwirtschaft e.V.)

title: Retrieve information on standard load profiles — slp_info
Retrieve information on standard load profiles — slp_info
text: https://www.bdew.de/energie/standardlastprofile-strom/ https://www.bdew.de/media/documents/2000131_Anwendung-repraesentativen_Lastprofile-Step--step.pdf https://www.bdew.de/media/documents/Zuordnung_der_VDEW-Lastprofile_zum_Kundengruppenschluessel.pdf

title: Retrieve information on standard load profiles — slp_info
text: profile_id load profile identifier, required language one "EN" (default) "DE"

title: Retrieve information on standard load profiles — slp_info
text: named list one element per profile_id. element list three character components: profile (identifier), description (short label), details (longer explanation).

title: Retrieve information on standard load profiles — slp_info
code: slp_info("G5", language = "DE") #> $G5 #> $G5$profile #> [1] "G5" #>  #> $G5$description #> [1] "Bäckerei mit Backstube" #>  #> $G5$details #> [1] "Bäckereien mit Backstube haben den Schwerpunkt ihres Verbrauchs an den Werktagen traditionell ab ca. 3 Uhr früh und in der Nacht zum Samstag ab etwa Mitternacht. Der Tagverbrauch ist zum Gesamtbedarf relativ gering und wird hauptsächlich von der Verkaufstätigkeit bestimmt. Verkaufsorientierte Bäckereien, in denen zu Geschäftszeiten Backwaren zubereitet werden ('Backen im Laden'), verhalten sich wie andere Läden und sind im Profil G4 einzuordnen." #>  #>   # multiple profile IDs are supported slp_info(c("G0", "G5")) #> $G0 #> $G0$profile #> [1] "G0" #>  #> $G0$description #> [1] "commerce in general" #>  #> $G0$details #> [1] "If an assignment to one of the profiles G1 to G6 is not possible or desired, this profile represents the weighted average of the overall group." #>  #>  #> $G5 #> $G5$profile #> [1] "G5" #>  #> $G5$description #> [1] "bakery with bakehouse" #>  #> $G5$details #> [1] "Bakeries with a bakehouse traditionally have their main consumption on weekdays from around 3 a.m. and on Saturday nights from around midnight. Daytime consumption is relatively low compared to overall demand and is mainly determined by sales activities. Sales-oriented bakeries in which bakery products are prepared during business hours ('in-store baking') behave like other stores and are classified in profile G4." #>  #>

what: standardlastprofile: BDEW Standard Load Profiles for Electricity — standardlastprofile-package
title: standardlastprofile: BDEW Standard Load Profiles for Electricity — standardlastprofile-package
text: Provides representative standard load profiles (SLPs) electricity published German Association Energy Water Industries (BDEW Bundesverband der Energie- und Wasserwirtschaft e.V.) tidy format. Covers 1999 profiles — households (H0), commerce (G0–G6), agriculture (L0–L2) — updated 2025 profiles (H25, G25, L25, P25, S25), additionally represent households photovoltaic systems battery storage. Also provides interface generating standard load profile user-defined date range. 1999 data methodology described VDEW (1999), "Repräsentative VDEW-Lastprofile", https://www.bdew.de/media/documents/1999_Repraesentative-VDEW-Lastprofile.pdf. generation algorithm described VDEW (2000), "Anwendung der Repräsentativen VDEW-Lastprofile step--step", https://www.bdew.de/media/documents/2000131_Anwendung-repraesentativen_Lastprofile-Step--step.pdf. 2025 profiles described BDEW (2025), "Standardlastprofile Strom", https://www.bdew.de/energie/standardlastprofile-strom/.

title: standardlastprofile: BDEW Standard Load Profiles for Electricity — standardlastprofile-package
text: Maintainer: Markus Döring m4rkus.doering@gmail.com [copyright holder]

title: standardlastprofile (development version)
text: Added five new standard load profiles published BDEW 2025: H25 (household), G25 (commerce), L25 (agriculture), P25 (combination profile photovoltaics), S25 (combination profile storage photovoltaics). five available via slp_generate() slp_info(), included slp dataset.

title: standardlastprofile (development version)
text: slp_generate() gains holidays argument: character Date vector ISO 8601 dates treated public holidays (mapped "sunday" algorithm). supplied, built-holiday data ignored entirely, giving callers full control dates count holidays.

title: standardlastprofile (development version)
text: state_code argument slp_generate() deprecated. Pass vector holiday dates new holidays argument instead (#3).

title: standardlastprofile (development version)
text: Fixed incorrect weekday assignment date range contains Dec 24 Dec 31 falls Sunday. previous check used () across entire range, caused every Dec 24/31 range mapped "workday" instead "saturday" (#2).

title: standardlastprofile 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-12-11 Initial CRAN release
